key_findings: HS receives inflows from IW service calls (~$0.18-0.31M/month) credited from Retail
HS receives inflows from EW parts reimbursement (~$0.17-0.32M/month) credited from Retail
PA merch disbursements hit HS (~$0.43-2.11M/month) netted against Retail credit
FY23 YTD: IW service calls total credit to HS = $2.64M, EW parts = $2.68M
Mike approach: interco PA debit to HS merch (net) = ~$(0.8)M to $(1.3)M/month in FY23

key_findings: Assurant pays ~68.9% of gross premiums (pct to Assurant)
Premium declining: Jul 2023 $5.1M vs Apr 2023 $8.2M after cancels
Outflows timing is 60-day lag from premium receipt
Oct 2023 shows $7.58M Assurant cash outflow (elevated vs typical $1.8M/month)
Contract count declining: 56,530 in Apr 2023 vs 30,442 in Jul 2023 (post HW wind-down)

hs_fy23_inflows_detail.note: Oct 24 shows $15.2M inflow day (likely Assurant profit share payment of $11.5M per notation)

bu_cash_contribution_analysis.source: BU beta sheet from Oct 2023 file (most complete FY23 YTD actuals through Sep 2023)
bu_cash_contribution_analysis.note: Inflows are receipts; disbursements are direct costs allocated. Net = rough cash contribution before shared corp costs.

commentary: Home Services: largest inflow BU ($502M FY23 YTD Feb-Sep), but also largest direct disbursement (-$191M). Net contribution highly positive for cash on inflows but heavy spend.
HTS (Home Technology Services): significant decline - $16.5M in Feb vs $9.0M in Sep; HTS prefund also declining ($2.6M -> $1.8M). Suggests HTS wind-down.
KCD: Near-zero inflows Jun-Sep ($2.7M, $0.02M, $1.3M, $0.2M) vs $5.8M in Feb. Major decline in KCD cash generation.
Monark: declining trend $4.6M in Feb to $1.6M in Sep.
Sears Stores (B2M): volatile but stable range $13-20M/month.
Cross Country HS (CCHS): very stable $4.7-5.4M/month - consistent cash contributor.
